codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and the current `src/model/stacking.py` / `tests/test_stacking.py` implementation. The feature adds the requested temporal XGBoost→LR stackers and uses out-of-fold base predictions, but two blocking defects remain: the trainer does not enforce the required `stacked_brier <= base_brier` gate on the normal CLI/default path, and CLI-produced joblib bundles are not reloadable across processes because `StackingEnsembleModel` is serialized as `__main__`.

issues.description: The trainer computes `base_brier` and `stacked_brier` (`src/model/stacking.py:369-386`) but then always saves the stacked artifact (`:389-399`) even when the stacker is worse than the base XGBoost model. Reproducing the supported CLI path on the feature's own synthetic fixture (`python -m src.model.stacking --training-data <temp> --output-dir <temp> --holdout-season 2025 --time-series-splits 4 --search-iterations 1 --random-state 17`) produced regressions for both shipped models: `f5_ml_stacking_model` worsened from 0.029656827449798584 to 0.072861816542493 Brier, and `f5_rl_stacking_model` worsened from 0.035920631140470505 to 0.07271633486597451. That means the implementation does not actually satisfy the feature's verification contract of `stacked_brier <= base_brier` on the default training path.

`StackingEnsembleModel` is a custom class that gets joblib-pickled at `src/model/stacking.py:399`, but the module never pins the class back to `src.model.stacking` when executed via `python -m src.model.stacking` (`:575-576`). As a result, CLI-generated bundles serialize the class as `__main__.StackingEnsembleModel` and cannot be loaded in a fresh interpreter. A separate-process `joblib.load()` of a CLI-produced `f5_ml_stacking_model_*.joblib` failed with `AttributeError: Can't get attribute 'StackingEnsembleModel' on <module '__main__'>`, which breaks the feature requirement to save a reusable stacking model.

The tests only assert Brier improvement for `f5_ml_stacking_model`, and they do so under a one-option `base_search_space` that intentionally weakens the base learner (`tests/test_stacking.py:73-76` and `:118-121`). That leaves the default search configuration and the RL stacker unprotected, which is why the holdout-regression bug above can slip through while the suite still passes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The new stacking trainer CLI is still not discoverable from `.factory/services.yaml` even though the manifest is described as the single source of truth for useful commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/services.yaml` lists install/lint/test/build commands plus pipeline entries for `daily`, `daily_dryrun`, `backtest`, and `calibrate_eval`, but no stacking-training command. This feature added a real CLI entrypoint at `src/model/stacking.py:285-317`, so future workers still have to rediscover `python -m src.model.stacking` manually.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `ml-worker` skill still does not explicitly reconcile its environment-init guidance with the Windows-specific setup notes already documented in the shared environment library, so workers keep recording justified procedure deviations for the same reason.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The handoff `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-19T23-22-14-583Z__t18-lr-stacking__cc344bcc-acf8-449d-b8b7-08c6e4b0ec35.json` marks `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` for step `1.2 Initialize Environment`, while `.factory/library/environment.md` already documents the Windows `bash .factory/init.sh` / `pip` gotcha and recommends the interpreter/manifest-based commands instead.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is no shared convention documenting that custom model classes written by `python -m ...` CLIs must pin `__module__` (and sometimes `sys.modules`) before joblib serialization. The later calibration feature had to rediscover and implement that pattern ad hoc, while stacking still misses it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `src/model/calibration.py:136-198` now sets `sys.modules.setdefault("src.model.calibration", ...)` and rewrites `__module__` for its custom model/calibrator classes, but neither `AGENTS.md` nor `.factory/library/` records that serialization rule. `src/model/stacking.py:156` and `:575-576` lack the equivalent safeguard, which matches the reload failure reproduced during this review.

summary: Fail. Commit `aa17431` adds the requested stacking trainer and OOF meta-features, but it still saves stackers even when they regress holdout Brier on the default path, and CLI-produced joblib bundles are not reloadable in a fresh interpreter because `StackingEnsembleModel` is pickled as `__main__`. I also noted shared-state gaps in the services manifest, `ml-worker` setup guidance, and the undocumented custom-model serialization convention.
